title: Domesday Book completed
summary: William I's commissioners record taxable manorial assets across most of England, organised by tenant-in-chief. Held at The National Archives, Kew.

title: Frome Channel diversion at St Augustine's Back, Bristol
summary: Bristol town authorities mobilise local labour to dig a trench diverting the River Frome through the marshlands of St Augustine's Back, creating a deep-water harbour and shifting Bristol's port capacity.

title: Shire Ditch (Red Earl's Dyke) cut along the Malvern ridge
summary: Linear ditch dug along the spine of the Malvern Hills following a boundary dispute over hunting rights between Gilbert de Clare (Earl of Gloucester) and Thomas de Cantilupe (Bishop of Hereford).

title: William of Worcester paced topographical survey of Bristol
summary: Worcester records street, church, wall and wharf dimensions of medieval Bristol by counting his own paces — one of the earliest detailed urban topographies in England.
source: MS Corpus Christi College Cambridge 210; printed editions e.g. James Dallaway (1834)

title: Kett's Rebellion / Mousehold Heath rising
summary: Robert Kett leads ~16,000 commoners onto Mousehold Heath outside Norwich, protesting enclosure of common lands. Suppressed by mercenary forces at the Battle of Dussindale; thousands killed.
source: Anthony Fletcher and Diarmaid MacCulloch, Tudor Rebellions

title: Cromwellian Act for the Settlement of Ireland
summary: Forced transplantation of indigenous Irish landholders westward across the Shannon — the policy paraphrased as 'To Hell or to Connacht' — clearing the eastern provinces for English / Scottish settlers.
source: Act for the Settlement of Ireland 1652; Down Survey records

title: Metropolitan Main Drainage Act / start of Bazalgette sewer network
summary: Following the 'Great Stink', Parliament authorises Joseph Bazalgette's interceptor sewer system. Construction runs 1859-1875 using ~318 million bricks; encloses the Fleet, Tyburn, Walbrook and Effra into underground conduits.
source: Stephen Halliday, The Great Stink of London

title: Giant's Causeway right-of-way ruling
summary: Belfast Court of Appeal hears the Antrim Coast Road Trustees / Causeway Co. dispute over public access; gates erected by the private syndicate are removed.

title: Great Penrhyn Quarry Strike begins
summary: Slate quarrymen at Penrhyn Quarry, Bethesda, begin a three-year strike against Lord Penrhyn — the longest industrial dispute in British history (1900-1903).
source: R. Merfyn Jones, The North Wales Quarrymen 1874-1922

title: Dinorwig pumped-storage power station commissioned
summary: 1,728 MW pumped-storage hydroelectric plant inaugurated within the abandoned Dinorwig slate quarry caverns near Llanberis, Gwynedd. Operated by First Hydro Co.
source: National Grid / First Hydro corporate records
